single.brief_rationale: The single agent run failed catastrophically with a 500 Internal Server Error before completing the core task. While it successfully loaded data and calculated the 99th percentile threshold, it did not execute the model evaluation, calculate metric deltas, or determine ranking changes. Consequently, it violates all three failure conditions in the reference hint (missing before/after experiments, deltas, and ranking statement). No visualizations were produced.

multi.brief_rationale: The multi-agent run successfully completed all task requirements through a structured workflow involving Planning, Research, Data Science, and Code Review stages. It explicitly calculated the 99th percentile, trimmed the dataset, computed RMSE/MAE/R2 for before/after scenarios, reported exact metric deltas (absolute and percentage), and clearly stated that model ranking did not change. The CodeReviewerAgent enforced contract compliance by blocking execution until all requirements were met. Three high-quality visualizations (boxplot, bar chart, slope chart) were generated to support the findings.

scores.comparison_notes: The multi-agent run is the definitive winner as it successfully completed the task while the single-agent run crashed before producing any results. The multi-agent workflow demonstrated superior robustness through explicit planning, research specification, and reviewer-gated validation, ensuring all reference hint constraints (deltas, ranking statement, before/after experiments) were satisfied. The single agent failed to meet any success criteria due to the runtime error, making the multi-agent approach the only viable solution in this comparison.
